sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_lists (id INT, nums LIST(INT), str_col STRING);
INSERT INTO test_lists VALUES (1, [1, 1, NULL, -3, 1, 5], 'example1'), (2, [2, NULL, 2, 3, 4], 'example2'), (3, [], 'example3');
SELECT id, array_distinct(nums) AS distinct_nums FROM test_lists WHERE array_distinct(nums) @> [1, 5];
SELECT id, nums FROM test_lists WHERE nums @> array_distinct([NULL, 1]);
SELECT id, array_distinct(nums) FROM test_lists WHERE array_distinct(nums) @> (SELECT array_distinct(nums) FROM test_lists WHERE str_col = 'example1');
CREATE TABLE temp_list AS SELECT array_distinct(nums) AS distinct_nums FROM test_lists GROUP BY nums HAVING COUNT(nums) > 0;
SELECT id FROM test_lists WHERE nums @> (SELECT array_distinct([1, 2, 3, NULL]) FROM temp_list);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_lists WHERE array_distinct(nums) @> [2] AND nums @> array_distinct([1, 1, NULL]);
SELECT id, array_distinct(nums) FROM (SELECT id, nums FROM test_lists WHERE nums IS NOT NULL) t WHERE array_distinct(nums) @> (SELECT array_distinct([1, NULL]) FROM test_lists);
SELECT id, nums FROM test_lists WHERE array_distinct(nums) @> (SELECT UNNEST(array_distinct([1, 2, 2, NULL, -3])));
